title: javascript part 2
content:
📘 DOM + JavaScript Notes (Hinglish) — Part 1

🔹 DOM kya hota hai? (Basic Understanding)

👉 DOM = Document Object Model

Simple language me:

  • Jo bhi HTML page tum banate ho

  • Wo objects ka structure hota hai

Example:

  • <html> → object

  • <head> → object

  • <body> → object

  • <div> → object

👉 Ye sab milke ek tree structure banate hain
👉 Isi ko bolte hain DOM

🔹 Important Concept

👉 “Agar tum DOM samajh gaye”:

To tum:

  • Data read kar sakte ho

  • Value change kar sakte ho

  • New elements add kar sakte ho

  • Elements delete kar sakte h

🔹 Window Object (Sabse Top Level)

👉 Browser me sab kuch ek global object ke andar hota hai:

  • Uska naam hai → window

💡 Simple samjho:

  • Browser = Window

  • Sab kuch uske andar hai

🔹 Structure Start hota hai yahan se

Hierarchy:

Window
  ↓
Document
  ↓
HTML
  ↓
Head + Body

🔹 Window ke andar kya hota hai?

Jab tum console me likhte ho:

console.log(window)

👉 Tumhe bahut saari cheezein dikhti hain:

  • location (tum kaha ho)

  • history

  • navigator

  • document

👉 Ye sab window ke properties hain

🔹 Important: document

👉 Sabse important object hai:

document

👉 Ye represent karta hai:

  • Tumhara pura HTML page

🔹 Shortcut Understanding

  • window.document ✔️

  • document ✔️ (direct use kar sakte ho)

👉 Kyun?

  • Kyunki browser automatically samajhta hai ki tum document use karoge

🔹 Document ke andar kya hota hai?

console.log(document)

👉 Tumhe milta hai:

  • doctype

  • html

  • head

  • body

👉 Ye hi actual DOM structure hai

🔹 DOM Tree ka Concept (Very Important 🔥)

👉 DOM ek tree structure hota hai

Structure:

Window
  ↓
Document
  ↓
HTML
  ├── Head
  │     ├── Title
  │     │     └── Text Node
  │     └── Meta (attributes only)
  │
  └── Body
        └── Div
              ├── H1
              │     ├── Attribute (Class name)
              │     └── Text Node
              │
              └── Paragraph
                    └── Text Node

🔹 Node kya hota hai?

👉 DOM me har cheez ko Node bola jata hai

Types:

  1. Element Node
    → <div>, <h1>, <p>

  2. Text Node
    → Jo text likha hai

  3. Attribute Node
    → class, id

🔹 Example Breakdown

<title>DOM Learning</title>

  • Title → Element Node

  • “DOM Learning” → Text Node

🔹 Parent-Child Concept

Example:

  • Div → Parent

  • H1 & P → Children

👉 Important for DOM navigation

🔹 Sibling Concept

Example:

  • H1 aur P
    👉 Dono same level pe hain → siblings

🔹 Real Use Case

Agar tumhe kisi element tak jana hai:

👉 Tumhe path pata hona chahiye:

window → document → html → body → div → p

🔹 Selection ka logic

Agar multiple div ho:

👉 Tumhe identify karna padega:

  • class

  • id

  • attribute

📘 DOM + JavaScript Notes (Hinglish) — Part 3

🔹 Real Browser me DOM kaise dekhein?

👉 Kisi bhi website pe jao
👉 Right click → Inspect

👉 Fir open karo:

  • Console tab

Observation

👉 Jab tum window open karte ho:

  • Bahut zyada data hota hai

👉 Jab tum document open karte ho:

  • Focused HTML structure milta hai

🔹 Advanced Console Methods 

DOCUMENT PURE PAGE KO PKD KE RKHTA H 

Normal log:

console.log(document)

👉 Limited view deta hai

Better debugging:

console.dir(document)

👉 Full properties show karta hai

🔹 Useful Properties

1. Base URL

document.baseURI

👉 Current page ka URL deta hai

2. Links Collection

document.links

👉 Page ke saare links deta hai

🔹 Important Concept ⚠️

👉 document.links kya return karta hai?

  • ❌ Array nahi

  • ✅ HTMLCollection

🔹 HTMLCollection vs Array

HTMLCollection = live collection (auto update hota hai)

👉 Difference:

FeatureHTMLCollectionArray
map()❌✅
forEach()❌✅
index access✅✅

🔹 Example

document.links[0]

👉 First link mil jayega

🔹 Conversion Trick

👉 Agar array banana ho:

Array.from(document.links)

👉 Ab tum map, filter use kar sakte ho

🔹 Element Select kaise karein?

👉 DOM ka main kaam = elements select karna

🔹 Method 1: getElementById ⭐

document.getElementById("firstHeading")

👉 Ye return karega:

  • pura HTML element

Example output:

<h1 id="firstHeading">...</h1>

🔹 Ab Manipulation start 🔥

👉 Ab hum element change kar sakte hain

🔹 innerHTML (Very Important)

document.getElementById("firstHeading").innerHTML = "Chai aur Code"

👉 Result:

  • Page ka heading change ho jayega

🔹 Kya hua internally?

👉 Tumne:

  • DOM access kiya

  • Element select kiya

  • Text replace kar diya

👉 Isko bolte hain:

👉 DOM Manipulation

🔹 Real Power 💥

Ab tum kar sakte ho:

  • Text change

  • HTML add/remove

  • Elements create

  • Elements delete                                  

🔹 Console Me Kaam Start

  • Browser me Console open kiya

  • Yahin se hum JavaScript DOM ke saath interact karenge

🔹 Element Select Karna (getElementById)

  • Sabse pehla method:

document.getElementById("title")

👉 Breakdown:

  • document → pura HTML page

  • .getElementById() → specific element ko select karega

  • "title" → id jo humne diya tha

⚠️ Important Syntax Note

  • Case sensitive hota hai:

    • getElementById (G, E, I capital)

🔹 Output Kya Mila?

  • Ye method return karta hai:
    👉 poora HTML element (object form me)

🔹 Object Se Data Kaise Nikaale?

  • Dot (.) use karke properties access karte hain

Example:

document.getElementById("title").id

👉 Output: "title"

🔹 Class Access Karna

document.getElementById("title").className

👉 Important:

  • HTML me → class

  • JavaScript me → className

⚠️ Concept Clear

  • JS me directly class nahi likhte

  • Always use:
    👉 className

🔹 getAttribute() Method

  • Kisi bhi attribute ki value lene ke liye:

document.getElementById("title").getAttribute("id")

👉 Output: "title"

document.getElementById("title").getAttribute("class")

👉 Output: "heading"

🔹 setAttribute() Method

  • Attribute change/set karne ke liye

document.getElementById("title").setAttribute("class", "test")

👉 Result:

  • Old class "heading" remove ho jayegi

  • New class "test" set ho jayegi

⚠️ Important Warning

  • setAttribute overwrite karta hai

  • Old value remove ho jaati hai

🔹 Multiple Classes Set Karna

document.getElementById("title").setAttribute("class", "test heading")

👉 Result:

  • Dono classes apply ho jayengi

🔹 Variable Me Store Karna

  • Easy access ke liye:

const title = document.getElementById("title")

👉 Ab baar-baar likhne ki zarurat nahi

🔹 Key Learning

  • Element mil gaya → ab usko:

    • read kar sakte ho

    • modify kar sakte ho

    • attributes change kar sakte ho

📒 Part 3 Notes (Style Manipulation + Styling via JS) – Hinglish

🔹 Element Already Stored

const title = document.getElementById("title")

👉 Ab title ek object hai jiske andar bahut properties hain

🔹 Style Property Access Karna

title.style

👉 Ye ek object hai jisme saari CSS properties hoti hain

🔹 CSS vs JS Syntax Difference

  • CSS me:

background-color

  • JS me:

backgroundColor

👉 camelCase use hota hai JS me

🔹 Background Color Change

title.style.backgroundColor = "green"

👉 Instant UI change ho jayega

🔹 Text Color Change

title.style.color = "white"

🔹 Padding Add Karna

title.style.padding = "15px"

🔹 Border Radius Add Karna

title.style.borderRadius = "10px"

🔹 Important Concept

  • style ek object hai

  • Har CSS property ko:
    👉 .propertyName = "value" se set karte hain

🔹 Live Change Effect

  • Jaise hi code run karte ho:
    👉 Browser me instant change visible

🔹 Multiple Styles Apply Karna

title.style.backgroundColor = "green"
title.style.color = "white"
title.style.padding = "15px"
title.style.borderRadius = "10px"

🔹 Key Learning

  • JS se styling karna = dynamic UI change

  • Ye hi real power hai DOM manipulation ki

📒 Part 4 Notes (Content Manipulation – textContent, innerText, innerHTML) – Hinglish

🔹 Content Nikalna (Text Access)

  • Element ka actual text lene ke liye:

title.textContent

👉 Output: pura text jo element ke andar hai

🔹 Similar Properties (3 Important)

  1. textContent

  2. innerText

  3. innerHTML

👉 Teeno similar lagte hain but difference hai ⚠️

🔸 1. textContent

title.textContent

👉 Ye kya karta hai:

  • Saara content de deta hai

  • Even hidden content bhi (CSS se hide ho toh bhi)

🔸 2. innerText

title.innerText

👉 Ye kya karta hai:

  • Sirf visible text deta hai

  • Jo screen pe dikh raha hai wahi milega

🔸 3. innerHTML

title.innerHTML

👉 Ye kya karta hai:

  • HTML ke saath content deta hai

  • Tags bhi include karta hai

🔹 Example Situation

  • Suppose:

<h1 id="title">
  Hello <span style="display:none;">Hidden</span>
</h1>

🔹 Difference समझो

  • textContent 👉 "Hello Hidden"

  • innerText 👉 "Hello"

  • innerHTML 👉 "Hello <span style='display:none;'>Hidden</span>"

🔹 Key Concept

  • textContent → full data (hidden + visible)

  • innerText → only visible

  • innerHTML → HTML structure bhi

🔹 Content Change Karna

👉 Text change kar sakte ho:

title.innerText = "New Text"

🔹 HTML Inject Karna

title.innerHTML = "<h2>New Heading</h2>"

👉 Ye:

  • pura content replace karega

  • new HTML render karega

⚠️ Important Warning

  • innerHTML dangerous ho sakta hai:

    • agar user input directly daal diya toh
      👉 XSS attack ho sakta hai

🔹 Real Use Cases

  • textContent → safe data fetch

  • innerText → UI-based changes

  • innerHTML → HTML insert karna

✅ Summary (Part 4)

  • 3 main properties samjhi

  • Hidden vs visible difference clear hua

  • Content read + change karna seekha

  • HTML insert karna bhi seekha

📒 Part 5 Notes (Query Selector + Advanced Selection) – Hinglish

🔹 Problem with Old Methods

  • getElementById → sirf id ke liye

  • getElementsByClassName → limited
    👉 Real-world me flexible nahi

  • This can do:

    • multiple classes

    • nested elements

    • attribute selectors

    • pseudo-classes

🔹 Solution: querySelector 🔥

document.querySelector("h1")

👉 Ye kya karta hai:

  • First matching element return karta hai

🔹 Important Rule

  • Ye hamesha:
    👉 sirf first element select karta hai

🔹 Multiple Elements Example

<h2>One</h2>
<h2>Two</h2>
<h2>Three</h2>
document.querySelector("h2")

👉 Output: "One" (first h2)

🔹 ID Select Karna (CSS Style)

document.querySelector("#title")

👉 # → id selector

🔹 Class Select Karna

document.querySelector(".heading")

👉 . → class selector

🔹 CSS Selectors Use Karna

👉 Same CSS syntax use hota hai:

document.querySelector("div p")

👉 matlab:

  • div ke andar jo p hai → select karo

🔹 Attribute Selector

document.querySelector("input[type='password']")

👉 specific element select kar sakte ho

🔹 querySelectorAll 🔥

document.querySelectorAll("h2")

👉 Ye kya karta hai:

  • saare matching elements deta hai

🔹 Return Type

  • querySelector → single element

  • querySelectorAll → NodeList

🔹 NodeList kya hota hai?

  • Array jaisa hota hai

  • But exact array nahi hota

🔹 NodeList Access

const items = document.querySelectorAll("h2")

items[0]  // first element
items[1]  // second element

🔹 Loop Use Karna (forEach)

items.forEach(function(item) {
    item.style.color = "red"
})

👉 Saare elements pe style apply ho jayega

🔹 Real Example (List)

const ul = document.querySelector("ul")
const li = ul.querySelector("li")

li.style.backgroundColor = "green"

cont h1  = documentqueryselectorall("h1")
h1 ke nodelist aayegi or saari ko print kar rhe honge 
h1.style.color = 'green'
ause direct allowed nhi hoga likhna we have to do this like
myh1[0].style.color = 'green'
aise krna pdega quese yeh nodelist derha h for each use kar skte ho 

templilist.forEach(fucntion (li) {
li.style.color = 'green'
})

saare ka krdegaa

Array.from(htmlcollection)

kisko bhi arrrayt me conver krdega


🔹 Chain Query (Important)

  • Parent se child select kar sakte ho:

ul.querySelector("li")

🔹 Text Change

li.innerText = "Five"

👉 Content change ho gaya

🔹 Key Learning

  • querySelector = powerful + flexible

  • CSS selectors use kar sakte ho

  • querySelectorAll = multiple elements handle

📒 Part 6 Notes (NodeList vs HTMLCollection + Array Conversion) – Hinglish

🔹 Ab Tak Kya Dekha?

  • querySelectorAll → NodeList देता है

  • getElementsByClassName → HTMLCollection देता है

👉 Dono same nahi hote ⚠️

🔸 NodeList kya hota hai?

const items = document.querySelectorAll("li")

👉 Features:

  • Array jaisa hota hai

  • Index se access kar sakte ho

  • forEach support karta hai ✅

items.forEach(item => {
    item.style.color = "red"
})

🔸 HTMLCollection kya hota hai?

const items = document.getElementsByClassName("list-item")

👉 Features:

  • Array jaisa dikhta hai

  • But:
    ❌ forEach directly support nahi karta
    ❌ map bhi nahi

🔹 Problem Example

items.forEach(item => {
    item.style.color = "green"
})

👉 ❌ Error aayega (HTMLCollection me forEach nahi hota)

🔸 Solution → Convert into Array 🔥

const arr = Array.from(items)

👉 Ab:

  • Pure array ban gaya

  • Saare methods use kar sakte ho

🔹 Loop Ab Kaam Karega

arr.forEach(item => {
    item.style.backgroundColor = "orange"
})

🔸 Important Difference (Simple Language)

FeatureNodeListHTMLCollection
forEach✅ Yes❌ No
map❌ No❌ No
Array jaisa✅✅
Direct loop easy✅❌

🔹 Key Concept

👉 Jab bhi:

  • querySelectorAll → easy (NodeList)

  • getElementsByClassName → convert karna padega

🔸 Real Mistake (Common)

❌ Log directly HTMLCollection pe loop laga dete hain
👉 Error aata hai

✔️ Correct way:

Array.from(collection).forEach(...)

🔹 Example Flow

const tempClassList = document.getElementsByClassName("list-item")

const convertedArray = Array.from(tempClassList)

convertedArray.forEach(li => {
    li.style.color = "blue"
})

🔹 Deep Insight

  • NodeList aur HTMLCollection:
    👉 pure arrays nahi hote

  • Isliye:
    👉 kuch methods missing hote hain

🔹 Best Practice

  • Modern JS me:
    👉 querySelectorAll prefer karo

  • Agar old method use kiya:
    👉 convert to array

📒 Part 7 Notes (Real Website Practice – Wikipedia DOM Manipulation) – Hinglish

🔹 Real Practice Start

  • Ab hum apni file se bahar nikle

  • Real website pe practice ki 👉 Wikipedia

👉 Reason:

  • Real-world DOM structure samajhna

  • Complex elements handle karna

🔹 Inspect Element

  • Kisi bhi heading pe right click → Inspect

  • Example:

    • Heading (h2, h3) select ki

🔹 All Headings Select Karna

document.querySelectorAll("h2")

👉 Output:

  • NodeList of all h2 elements

🔹 Variable Me Store

const headings = document.querySelectorAll("h2")

🔹 First Element Access

headings[0].innerText

👉 Example output:

  • "History"

  • "Trademark"

🔹 Multiple Elements Manipulate Karna

👉 Loop lagate hain:

headings.forEach(function(h) {
    h.style.color = "red"
})

👉 Result:

  • Saare headings red ho gaye 🔴

🔹 Background + Padding Add

headings.forEach(function(h) {
    h.style.backgroundColor = "green"
    h.style.padding = "10px"
})

🔹 Content Change Karna

headings.forEach(function(h) {
    h.innerText = "Hitesh"
})

👉 Result:

  • Saare headings ka text change 😄

🔹 Important Observation ⚠️

  • Kabhi extra elements bhi select ho jaate hain
    👉 Kyun?

  • Same tag (h2) multiple jagah use hota hai

🔹 Better Targeting (Class Use)

document.querySelectorAll(".mw-headline")

👉 Ye specific class hai Wikipedia headings ki

🔹 Store + Loop

const heads = document.querySelectorAll(".mw-headline")

heads.forEach(function(h) {
    h.style.color = "black"
    h.style.backgroundColor = "green"
    h.style.padding = "5px"
})

🔹 Key Learning

  • Real websites me:
    👉 exact selector choose karna important hai

  • Sirf tag se kaam nahi chalta
    👉 class/id better hota hai

🔹 Powerful Concept

👉 Jo humne console me kiya:

  • Same code hum:

    • <script> tag me likh sakte hain

    • ya external JS file me

🔹 Real Use Case

  • Database se data aaya

  • Loop lagaya

  • Existing elements update kiye

👉 Ye sab same concept se hota hai

🎯 Ab tum kya kar sakte ho:

  • Apni website me dynamic changes

  • Data ko UI me inject karna

  • Basic projects banana

🔹 Topic: Introduction + Parent & Children in DOM

🧠 1. DOM ka basic idea

  • DOM = Document Object Model

  • Ye ek tree structure hota hai (like parent → child → sibling)

  • Har HTML element ek node hota hai

👉 Example structure:

<body>
  <div class="parent">
    <div class="day">Monday</div>
    <div class="day">Tuesday</div>
  </div>
</body>

🧠 2. Parent Element select karna

const parent = document.querySelector('.parent')

✔️ Ye .parent div ko select karega

🧠 3. Children access karna

parent.children

  • Ye return karta hai: HTMLCollection

  • Ye array jaisa hota hai (but exact array nahi)

👉 Example output:

HTMLCollection(4) [div.day, div.day, div.day, div.day]

🧠 4. Children count (length)

parent.children.length

✔️ Kitne child elements hain → return karega

🧠 5. Specific child access karna

parent.children[0]

✔️ First child (Monday)

parent.children[1].innerHTML

✔️ Output: "Tuesday"

🧠 6. Loop through children

for (let i = 0; i < parent.children.length; i++) {
  console.log(parent.children[i].innerHTML)
}

✔️ Output:

Monday
Tuesday
Wednesday
Thursday

🧠 7. Styling child element

parent.children[0].style.color = "orange"

✔️ First element ka color change ho jayega

🧠 8. Important properties

👉 First child element

parent.firstElementChild

✔️ First child return karega //thursfay 

👉 Last child element

parent.lastElementChild

✔️ Last child return karega

🧠 9. Key understanding

  • .children → only element nodes

  • Array jaisa behave karta hai

  • Loop easily chal sakta hai

⚡ Important Concept

👉 DOM traversal ka matlab:

  • Parent → Child

  • Child → Parent

  • Sibling → Next / Previous

Ye sab aage React samajhne me help karega 🚀


🧠 10. Child se Parent pe jaana

Agar aapne koi child element select kiya hai:

const dayOne = document.querySelector('.day')

👉 Ab parent pe jaana ho:

dayOne.parentElement

✔️ Ye uska parent return karega (i.e. .parent div)

⚡ Important point

  • Baar-baar querySelector use karna expensive hota hai

  • Direct relation use karna better hai:

    • parentElement

    • nextElementSibling

🧠 11. Sibling access (Next bhai 😄)

dayOne.nextElementSibling

✔️ Output: "Tuesday"

👉 Matlab:

  • Monday → next sibling → Tuesday

🧠 Related properties

  • nextElementSibling → next element

  • previousElementSibling → previous element

🧠 12. DOM = Tree traversal (DSA connection 🔥)

  • DOM ek tree structure hai

  • Jab aap:

    • parent → child jaate ho

    • child → parent jaate ho

    • sibling access karte ho

👉 basically aap tree traverse kar rahe ho

🧠 13. childNodes (Most confusing but important ⚠️)

parent.childNodes

👉 Ye return karta hai: NodeList

🤯 Surprise:

Agar 4 div hain, fir bhi count zyada aa sakta hai

parent.childNodes.length

👉 Output ho sakta hai: 9 (not 4 😲)

❓ Why?

Kyuki childNodes include karta hai:

  • text nodes (line breaks, spaces)

  • comments

  • elementsjj

🧠 Example:

<div class="parent">
  <div>Monday</div>
  <div>Tuesday</div>
</div>

👉 Browser internally isko count karta hai:

  • line break → text node

  • div → element node

  • line break → text node

🔥 Result:

NodeList(5) [text, div, text, div, text]

🧠 14. NodeList vs HTMLCollection

FeatureHTMLCollectionNodeList
Source.children.childNodes
IncludesOnly elementsEverything (text, comment, element)
Array-likeYesYes
LoopLimitedMore flexible

⚡ Key Difference

👉 .children:

  • clean data (only elements)

👉 .childNodes:

  • raw DOM structure (including spaces, comments)

🧠 15. Important Insight (Interview level 🔥)

  • DOM sirf visible elements nahi hota

  • Hidden nodes bhi exist karte hain:

    • spaces

    • line breaks

    • comments

👉 Isi wajah se:

  • Performance impact hota hai

  • React internally optimize karta hai (Virtual DOM)

🧠 16. Example: Comment bhi node hota hai

<!-- this is comment -->

👉 Ye bhi count hota hai childNodes me

⚡ Experiment

  • Ek extra line break add karo → nodes increase

  • Comment add karo → nodes increase

🧠 17. Summary of Traversal

✔️ Parent → Child

parent.children

✔️ Child → Parent

child.parentElement

✔️ Sibling

child.nextElementSibling

✔️ Raw Nodes

parent.childNodes

🎯 Important Takeaway

👉 DOM = Tree
👉 Har cheez node hai
👉 .children vs .childNodes samajhna very important

🔹 Topic: Creating Elements in DOM (createElement + attributes)

🧠 18. New Element create karna

👉 DOM me new element banane ke liye:

const div = document.createElement('div')

✔️ Ye ek new div create karega (memory me)
❌ Abhi page pe visible nahi hoga

⚡ Important

👉 Element sirf memory me create hota hai
👉 Page pe tab dikhega jab usko attach karenge

🧠 19. Check element (inspect)

Agar console me dekho:

console.log(div)

✔️ Output: <div></div>

🧠 20. Class add karna

div.className = "main"

✔️ Class add ho gayi

🧠 21. ID add karna

div.id = "myId"

🎲 Random ID (fun concept 😄)

div.id = Math.round(Math.random() * 10 + 1)

✔️ Har refresh pe new ID generate hogi

🧠 22. Custom Attribute add karna

div.setAttribute("title", "generated title")

✔️ Title attribute add ho gaya

⚡ Difference

  • div.id = ... → direct property

  • setAttribute() → generic method (recommended for custom attributes)

🧠 23. Styling add karna


div.style.backgroundColor = "green"
div.style.padding = "10px"

//abhi tk kuch bhi page pe nhi dikh rha hoga

✔️ CSS JS se apply ho gaya

🧠 24. Text add karna (Method 1 ❌ less optimal)

div.innerText = "Chai aur Code"

⚠️ Problem:

  • Ye pehle old value fetch karta hai

  • Fir overwrite karta hai

👉 Slightly inefficient // ek roundtrip extra h 

🧠 25. Text add karna (Method 2 ✅ better)

which is preferable 
const text = document.createTextNode("Chai aur Code")
// phle bananya 
div.appendChild(text)
// then add kiya

✔️ Ye direct text node create karta hai
✔️ More optimized approach

🧠 Concept

  • Text bhi ek node hota hai

  • (Part 2 me nodeList me dekha tha 🔥)

🧠 26. Append text to div

appendChild kya krta h us person ke andar as a child add krdet ah 
div.appendChild(text)

✔️ Ab div ke andar text aa gaya

🧠 27. Final Step (MOST IMPORTANT 🔥)

👉 Abhi tak sab memory me hai

👉 Page pe show karne ke liye:

dom me attach krne ke liye 

document.body.appendChild(div)

✔️ Ab element web page pe visible ho jayega 🎉

🧠 28. Full Code (Complete flow)

const div = document.createElement('div')

div.className = "main"
div.id = Math.round(Math.random() * 10 + 1)

div.setAttribute("title", "generated title")

div.style.backgroundColor = "green"
div.style.padding = "10px"

const text = document.createTextNode("Chai aur Code")
div.appendChild(text)

document.body.appendChild(div)

🎯 Final Understanding

👉 Steps:

  1. Element create karo

  2. Attributes set karo

  3. Text add karo

  4. DOM me attach karo

⚡ Real-world Use

  • API se data aaya → UI pe show karna

  • Cards, buttons, list dynamically banana

  • React bhi internally ye hi karta hai (optimized way)

🧠 Important Insight

👉 Jab tak:

document.body.appendChild(...)

nahi karoge → element visible nahi hoga

✅ Part 3 complete (FINAL PART 🎉)

📘 DOM Notes – Part 1 (Intro + Setup + Basic Add)

🔹 Video Context

  • Ye DOM series ka final video hai
  • Goal: Tum confident ho jao projects banane ke liye
  • Focus:
    • DOM manipulation
    • Selectors practice
    • Elements add/remove/edit

🔹 Target: New List Item Add karna

💡 Idea:

  • Multiple languages add karni hai in that list
  • Isliye function bana lete hain (reusable) jisse me direct kar parha hounga  

🔹 Function: Add Language (Basic Method)

function addLanguage(langName) {
  const li = document.createElement('li')
  li.innerHTML = '${langName}'
  document.querySelector('.language').appendChild(li)
}

🔍 Step-by-step samjho:

1. Element create

const li = document.createElement('li')

👉 New <li> create hua (abhi DOM me nahi hai)

2. Value add

li.innerHTML = langName

👉 Text insert kiya (e.g., "Python")

3. Parent select

document.querySelector('.language')

👉 <ul class="language"> mil gaya

4. Append child

.appendChild(li)

👉 New <li> list ke end me add ho gaya

🔹 Function Call

addLanguage("Python")
addLanguage("TypeScript")

👉 Output:

  • JavaScript
  • Python
  • TypeScript

⚠️ Important Concept

❌ innerHTML Problem:

  • Har baar DOM poora traverse hota hai
  • Large apps me performance issue

🧠 Key Takeaway (Part 1)

  • createElement() → element banata hai
  • innerHTML → content add karta hai
  • querySelector() → element select karta hai
  • appendChild() → DOM me attach karta hai

👉 Ye basic approach hai (but not optimized)


Ab hum same kaam (list item add) karenge but optimized way me ✅
(ye interview + real projects me important hota hai)

🔹 Problem with Previous Method

li.innerHTML = langName

❌ Issue:

  • Browser ko poora DOM tree traverse karna padta hai
  • Large apps me slow ho sakta hai

🔹 Optimized Function

function addOptiLanguage(langName) {
  const li = document.createElement('li')
  li.appendChild(document.createTextNode(langName))
  document.querySelector('.language').appendChild(li)
}

🔍 Step-by-step Breakdown

1. Create Element (Same as before)

const li = document.createElement('li')

2. Create Text Node (NEW 🔥)

document.createTextNode(langName)

👉 Ye ek text node banata hai (HTML parse nahi karta)

3. Append Text Node inside <li>

li.appendChild(document.createTextNode(langName))

👉 Direct text inject ho gaya (safe + fast)

4. Append <li> to <ul>

document.querySelector('.language').appendChild(li)


🔹 Function Call

addOptiLanguage("GoLang")

👉 Output me new item add ho jayega ✅

⚡ Why this is Better?

MethodBehavior
innerHTML ❌Full DOM re-parse
createTextNode ✅Direct insertion

👉 Isliye:

  • Faster 🚀
  • Safer (no HTML injection issues)

🔹 Extra Concept: Scope

👉 Dono functions me same variable name use hua (li)

❓ Problem kyu nahi?

  • Function scope alag hota hai
  • Variables bahar access nahi hote

🧠 Key Takeaway (Part 2)

  • createTextNode() → best practice for text insert
  • appendChild() → efficient DOM update
  • Avoid innerHTML in large projects

📘 DOM Notes – Part 3 (Edit / Replace Elements)

Ab hum seekhenge 👉 existing values ko edit / replace kaise kare
(ye real projects me bahut use hota hai)

🔹 Step 1: Element Select karna

const secondLang = document.querySelector("li:nth-child(2)")

✔ Samjho:

  • li:nth-child(2) → second <li> select karega

  • Example:

    1. JavaScript
    2. Python 👈 (ye select hoga)

🔹 Method 1: innerHTML se Edit ❌

secondLang.innerHTML = "Mojo"

✔ Result:

  • Python → Mojo ho jayega

❌ Problem:

  • Same issue: DOM re-render / traverse
  • Not optimized

🔹 Method 2: Replace Entire Element (Best ✅)

Step-by-step:

const newLi = document.createElement('li')
newLi.textContent = "Mojo"
secondLang.replaceWith(newLi)

🔍 Breakdown:

1. New Element Create

const newLi = document.createElement('li')

2. Text Add

newLi.textContent = "Mojo"

👉 textContent vs innerHTML

text content  ==  appendchild

  • textContent = safer & faster

3. Replace Old with New

secondLang.replaceWith(newLi)

👉 Old <li> remove + new add


yeh nhi ki yeh sbse best h some paces inner html hi shi h isse textcontent se 

🔹 Method 3: outerHTML se Replace

const firstLang = document.querySelector("li:first-child")
firstLang.outerHTML = "<li>TypeScript</li>"

✔ Result:

  • JavaScript → TypeScript

⚠️ Note:

  • outerHTML → pura element replace karta hai
  • Isme aapko full HTML string deni padti hai

👉 Isliye:

  • Easy hai 👍
  • But safe/clean code nahi ❌

🔹 Comparison (Important 🔥)

MethodUse CaseRecommended
innerHTMLQuick change❌
textContentText update✅
replaceWithFull replace✅🔥
outerHTMLDirect HTML replace⚠️

🧠 Key Takeaway (Part 3)

  • querySelector() powerful selector hai
  • replaceWith() = clean & modern approach
  • textContent > innerHTML


Ab last part 🔥
👉 Elements delete / remove kaise karte hain

li:first-child and li:last-child saare  nhi dega specific dega

🔹 Step 1: Last Element Select karo

const lastLang = document.querySelector("li:last-child")

✔ Samjho:

  • li:last-child → last <li> select karega

  • Example:

    JavaScript
    Python
    TypeScript 👈 (ye select hoga)

🔹 Step 2: Remove Element

lastLang.remove()

✔ Result:

  • Last item delete ho jayega

🔹 Alternative: Specific Element Remove

const firstLang = document.querySelector("li:first-child")
firstLang.remove()

👉 First item delete ho jayega

🔥 Important Concept

✔ Remove karne ke liye:

  • Pehle element ka reference lena जरूरी है
  • Fir .remove() call karo

🧠 Simple Flow

  1. Select element
  2. Call .remove()

👉 Done ✅

🎯 FINAL DOM SUMMARY (पूरी वीडियो का सार)

🔹 1. Create Elements

document.createElement('li')

🔹 2. Add Content

MethodUse
innerHTML ❌Avoid
textContent ✅Best
createTextNode ✅Optimized

🔹 3. Add to DOM


parent.appendChild(child)

🔹 4. Select Elements

SelectorMeaning
.classclass select
#idid select
li:nth-child(2)2nd element
li:first-childfirst
li:last-childlast

🔹 5. Edit Elements

MethodUse
innerHTML ❌avoid
textContent ✅best
replaceWith ✅full replace
outerHTML ⚠️direct replace

🔹 6. Remove Elements

element.remove()

🚀 Final Understanding

👉 Tum ab ye sab kar sakte ho:

  • Elements create ✔
  • Add ✔
  • Edit ✔
  • Replace ✔
  • Remove ✔

👉 Matlab:
DOM basics = DONE ✅


🟢 PART 1: Basic Setup + HTML Structure

  • Ek HTML file banayi → one.html
  • Basic structure:
    • <h2> → "Amazing Images"
    • <ul> → multiple <li>
    • Har <li> ke andar image
    • Har image ko ID di gayi (jaise: photoshop, japan, river, etc.)
    • Last me ek Google link bhi diya
  • Thodi basic styling:
    • Background → dark (#414141)
    • Text → aliceblue
    • Links ka color bhi change kiya

👉 Goal: JavaScript Events samajhna (HTML styling nahi)

👉 proceed

🟢 PART 2: JavaScript Events Introduction

  • JS me events sequentially run hote hain
  • BUT browser events user activity pe depend karte hain
    • Click
    • Mouse move
    • Keyboard press
    • Drag & drop

👉 Matlab:

Code tab chalega jab koi action hoga

👉 proceed

🟢 PART 3: 3 Ways to Add Events

❌ 1. Inline HTML Event (Bad Practice)

<img onclick="alert('hello')">
  • Kaam karta hai ✅
  • BUT scalable nahi ❌
  • Maintain karna mushkil ❌

⚠️ 2. JS me onclick

document.getElementById("owl").onclick = function(){
  alert("clicked");
}
  • Better than inline 👍
  • But limited features ❌

✅ 3. Best Approach: addEventListener

document.getElementById("owl")
  .addEventListener("click", function(){
    alert("clicked");
  });
  • Multiple events handle kar sakta hai
  • More control
  • Modern approach 🔥


upar wala jb click hoga jbhum images pe click kreng e; or ncieh wala jb click hoga jb hum specifcally owl pe click krenge
toh agar hum kis bhi images pe clik krenge toh aayega click inside the ul 
and specffiallcly owl wali pe krunga toh phle owl clicked aayega fir click inside the ul aayega
isi flow ko kha h event propogation (means bubbling)

🟢 PART 4: Event Object (Most Important)

.addEventListener("click", function(event){
  console.log(event);
})

👉 Event object me milta hai:

  • type → event ka type (click, keypress)
  • timestamp → kab hua
  • target → kis element pe hua
  • clientX, clientY → mouse position
  • altKey, ctrlKey, shiftKey → keys pressed
  • srcElement
  • currentTarget

👉 Interview me use:

  • Click location
  • Time tracking
  • Dynamic UI

👉 proceed

🟢 PART 5: Event Propagation (Core Concept)

👉 2 types:

1. Bubbling (Default) (inside se outside jaaata h )

  • Inside → Outside
  • Child → Parent

Example:

IMG → LI → UL

👉 Pehle image ka event
👉 fir parent ka

isme ek caputring mode aata h jo ise pura ulta hota h phle image wala krega fir specific wala krega

2. Capturing (just last wala jo by default false hota h use true krna h )

  • Outside → Inside
  • Parent → Child 
addEventListener("click", fn, true)

👉 true = capturing mode

false = capturing mode off

👉 proceed

🟢 PART 6: stopPropagation()

👉 Problem:
Event upar tak propagate ho jata hai (bubbling)

agar aap nhi hcahte ki event aage tk jaaye toh aap stop propgation krdenge

sirf own clicked hi aayega par ke iamges me jaayega

👉 Solution:

event.stopPropagation();

👉 Use:

  • Jab child aur parent dono pe event hai
  • Aur tum nahi chahte parent trigger ho

👉 proceed

🟢 PART 7: preventDefault()

👉 Default behavior rokne ke liye

Example:

  • Link click → page open hota hai
  • Form submit → reload hota hai 
event.preventDefault();

👉 Example:

document.getElementById("google")
.addEventListener("click", function(e){
  e.preventDefault();
});

👉 Result:
Google link click karne pe redirect nahi hoga ( jo unka default kaam tha woh humne prevent kardiya )

👉 proceed

  • e.target → kis pe click hua (image dega )

🟢 FINAL SUMMARY

  • Inline events ❌ avoid
  • onclick ⚠️ limited
  • addEventListener ✅ best
  • Event Object = GOLD 🔥
  • Bubbling (default)
  • Capturing (rare use)
  • stopPropagation() → bubbling stop
  • preventDefault() → default behavior stop
  • Real-world:

📘 JavaScript Async (Asynchronous Code) – Notes (Part 1)

🔹 1. Video Overview

  • ये पूरा topic theoretical है
  • Goal:
    • Async code समझना
    • Foundation build करना
    • आगे Promises, async/await आसानी से समझ आ जाए

🔹 2. JavaScript Basics (Very Important)

✅ JavaScript by default:

  • Synchronous होती है (line by line) (ek ke baad ek )
  • Single-threaded होती है (slow kh skte ho) ( but dont feel like slow)

🔸 Synchronous क्या होता है?

  • Code line-by-line execute होता है
  • Next line तब तक execute नहीं होती जब तक previous complete न हो जाए

👉 Example:

console.log(1);
console.log(2);
console.log(3);

➡ Output: 1 → 2 → 3 (sequence maintain)

🔸 Single-threaded क्या होता है?

  • JavaScript में only 1 thread होता है
  • मतलब:
    • एक समय पर सिर्फ 1 task execute होगा

❗ Important Point:

  • JS slow लग सकती है compared to multi-threaded languages
  • BUT:
    • JS smart है क्योंकि ये delegation use करती है
    • यानी कुछ काम बाहर भेज देती है (browser / runtime को)

🔹 3. Runtime Environment

JavaScript अकेले काम नहीं करती ❌
हमेशा किसी environment में चलती है:

🔸 Examples:

  • Browser
  • Node.js

👉 यहाँ extra features मिलते हैं:

  • Web APIs (browser में)
  • APIs (Node.js में)

🔹 4. Execution Context (Short Recap)

जब JS code चलता है:

  • Execution context बनता है

इसमें होता है:

  1. Memory Heap → variables store होते हैं
  2. Call Stack → functions execute होते हैं

🔸 Call Stack कैसे काम करता है?

  • Stack structure (LIFO - Last In First Out)

👉 Example:

main()
  -> func1()
     -> func2()

Execution:

  • func2 → func1 → main

🔸 Important Rule:

👉 Each operation waits for previous one to complete

➡ यही synchronous behavior है

🔹 5. Blocking vs Non-Blocking Code

🔸 Blocking Code

  • Program execution रुक जाता है
  • जब तक task complete न हो जाए

👉 Real-life example:

  • तुमसे कहा: “बैठे रहो, कुछ मत करो”
    ➡ तुम stuck हो गए

🔸 Non-Blocking Code

  • Program रुकता नहीं है
  • बाकी काम चलता रहता है

👉 Example:

  • “तुम अपना काम करो, मैं पानी लेकर आता हूँ”

🔹 6. Real Problem (File Reading)

❗ Biggest blocking task:

  • File read करना

क्यों?

  • Direct access नहीं मिलता
  • OS (Operating System) involve होता है

Flow:

  1. Program → request भेजता है
  2. OS kernel → file read करता है
  3. Response वापस देता है

➡ तब तक program wait करता है (blocking)

jb tk program hold hojata h kuch or kar nhi skta 

🔹 7. Sync vs Async File Reading

🔸 Sync (Blocking)

  • File read होने तक program रुक जाएगा

🔸 Async (Non-blocking)

  • Program चलता रहेगा ( baaki ka kaam hota rhega )
  • File ready होने पर callback मिलेगा

🔹 8. Important Insight ⚠️

❌ "Non-blocking हमेशा best है" → WRONG (humesha best nahi h )
✅ "Depends on use-case"
in case of login (database me check krne se phle confirm kardiya ) ( iskeliye toh block hona hi chaiye ) 

🔸 Example (Wrong Use Case)

  • User registration:
    1. DB में data save करना
    2. Success message भेजना

👉 अगर async किया:

  • Message पहले चला गया
  • DB fail हो गया 😬

🔸 Correct Approach:

  • पहले DB save complete करो (blocking style)
  • फिर message भेजो

🔥 Conclusion (Part 1)

  • JS = synchronous + single-threaded
  • Blocking vs Non-blocking depends on use-case
  • Async code = performance improve करने का तरीका
  • लेकिन blindly use नहीं करना

🔹 9. JavaScript Engine क्या होता है?

👉 JS Engine = वो component जो JS code execute करता है

इसके main parts:

  1. Memory Heap
    • Variables store होते हैं
  2. Call Stack
    • Functions execute होते हैं

➡️ यही actual “JavaScript Engine” है

🔹 10. Problem कहाँ आती है?

👉 JS engine:

  • Single-threaded है
  • सब कुछ खुद नहीं कर सकता

👉 इसलिए:

  • External help लेता है (Browser / Node.js)

🔹 11. Web APIs क्या होते हैं?

👉 ये features JS के नहीं होते, बल्कि Browser provide करता है

🔸 Examples:

  • setTimeout()
  • setInterval()
  • DOM events (click, load, etc.)
  • fetch()

👉 Important:

  • Browser में → Web APIs
  • Node.js में → APIs (but DOM नहीं होता)
task quueue bhi hota h voh bhi javascrupt ko fast or asynchronus bnata h 

🔹 12. Complete Async System (Big Picture)

Async system में ये चीजें होती हैं:

  1. Call Stack
  2. Web APIs
  3. Task Queue
  4. Event Loop
agar m chahta hu ki kuch aisa ho mera kaam kre or us kaam ko ;hone ke baadd yaad dilade yeh isis se hota h 

🔹 13. Flow of Async Code (Step-by-Step)

🔸 Step 1: Code Call Stack में आता है

  • JS line-by-line execute करता है

🔸 Step 2: Async function detect होता है

👉 Example:

do side baad hello dena h yeh register call back me store hojata h ; isse kya hoga ki ; 

voh rewgister krtta h ise 2 second baad cla krna ise tb krna jb button dabe aise aise

setTimeout(() => {
  console.log("Hello");
}, 2000);

➡️ ये call stack से हटकर Web API में चला जाता है

🔸 Step 3: Web API handle करता है

  • Timer start होता है (2 sec)
  • JS engine free हो जाता है

🔸 Step 4: Callback ready होने पर

  • Callback → Task Queue में जाता है

🔸 Step 5: Event Loop check करता है

👉 Event Loop का काम:

  • Call Stack empty है या नहीं check करना

🔸 Step 6: Execution

  • अगर Call Stack empty है:
    • Task Queue से callback उठाकर execute करता है
    • yeh queue line me lgati h jo jo kaam hone wale hote h 
    • task queue saari chije call stack me daaldeta h 

🔹 14. Task Queue (Callback Queue)

👉 Queue structure (FIFO)

  • First In → First Out

👉 Example:

  • McDonald's line 🍔
  • पहले आया → पहले serve

🔹 15. Event Loop (Most Important 🔥)

👉 Event Loop = brain of async system

इसका काम:

  • Continuously check करना:
    • Call Stack empty है या नहीं
  • अगर empty है:
    • Task Queue से function उठाओ
    • Execute करो

🔹 16. Example Flow (Important)

console.log(1);

setTimeout(() => { ( set time out likht e hi call bnek web api me chlagya iske wjh se queue me yeh 3 ke baad lgega 
  console.log(2);
}, 0);

console.log(3);

🔸 Step-by-step execution:

  1. console.log(1) → print → ✅
  2. setTimeout → Web API में चला गया
  3. console.log(3) → print → ✅
  4. Callback → Task Queue में आया
  5. Event Loop → Call Stack empty देखा
  6. Callback execute → 2 print

✅ Final Output:

1
3
2

🔥 Important Trick Question

👉 setTimeout(..., 0) का मतलब:

  • तुरंत execute ❌
  • minimum delay के बाद execute ✔️

➡️ क्योंकि:

  • पहले पूरा call stack empty होगा

🔹 17. Callback Registration

👉 जब तुम async काम करते हो:

  • तुम actually callback register कर रहे होते हो

Examples:

  • setTimeout
  • setInterval
  • button click
  • event listeners

🔹 18. Task Queue vs Call Stack

FeatureCall StackTask Queue
StructureStack (LIFO)Queue (FIFO)
कामExecute codeWait for turn
PriorityHighLow

🔹 19. Promises & Priority Queue (Intro)

isme fetch use htoa h jo promise pe work krta h ki hua ho toh bhi btandena nhi hua toh bhi btadena 

👉 कुछ async चीजें high priority होती हैं

Example:

  • Promises
  • fetch()

👉 इनके लिए अलग queue होती है:

  • Microtask Queue (High Priority)

🔹 20. Priority Rule ⚠️

👉 Execution order:

  1. Call Stack
  2. Microtask Queue (Promises)
  3. Task Queue (Callbacks)

🔥 Final Understanding (Part 2)

👉 Complete flow:

  1. Code → Call Stack
  2. Async → Web APIs
  3. Done → Task Queue / Microtask Queue
  4. Event Loop → Stack empty check
  5. Execute

🧠 Easy याद रखने का तरीका:

👉 “Stack → API → Queue → Loop → Stack”

🔥 Conclusion

  • Async code = illusion of parallelism
  • JS actually single-threaded ही है
  • Event Loop ही magic करता है
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

🔹 Intro: Time Functions kya hote hain?

  • Ye normal JS functions nahi hote
  • Ye browser-based APIs hote hain
  • Matlab:
    • Ye JS engine ka direct part nahi hain
    • Ye browser provide karta hai

👉 Example:

  • setTimeout
  • setInterval

🔹 Important Concept

  • JS me ek call stack hota hai
  • Jab tum setTimeout ya setInterval use karte ho:
    • Ye direct run nahi hota
    • Ye browser ke paas chala jata hai
    • Fir event loop ke through wapas aata hai

👉 Isliye:

  • Ye async behavior create karta hai

🔹 Basic HTML Setup

Simple page banaya:

  • ek <h1> → "Chai aur Code"
  • ek button → "Stop" 
<h1>Chai aur Code</h1>
<button id="stop">Stop</button>

👉 Console open karke output check karte hain

🔹 setTimeout kya hota hai?

yeh aise function h jo browser ke through milta h 

📌 Definition:

  • setTimeout ek function hai jo:
    • specific time ke baad ek baar run hota hai

📌 Syntax: sbse chahiye handler chahiye (callback) dusra chahiye timeout () 

setTimeout(callback, time_in_ms)

📌 Example:

setTimeout(function(){
    console.log("Hitesh")
}, 2000)

👉 Output:

  • 2 seconds baad → "Hitesh" print hoga
  • Sirf ek baar run hoga
ek set interval() hota h jo ki kya htoa h ki har 2 second ke baad print karega

🔹 Important Terms

1. Callback / Handler

  • Jo function tum pass karte ho → wo callback hota hai 
function(){
    console.log("Hitesh")
}

👉 Isko hi handler bhi bolte hain

2. Time (ms me hota hai)

  • 1000 ms = 1 second
  • 2000 ms = 2 second

🔹 Function Reference vs Execution

❌ Galat:

setTimeout(sayHello(), 2000)

👉 Ye turant execute ho jayega

✅ Sahi:

setTimeout(sayHello, 2000)

👉 Ye reference pass karta hai

Example:

function sayHello(){
    console.log("Hitesh")
}

setTimeout(sayHello, 2000)

🔹 Real Use Case (DOM Change)

Goal:

  • 2 sec baad text change karna

Step 1: Function banao

function changeText(){
    document.querySelector("h1").innerHTML = "Best JS Series"
}

Step 2: setTimeout use karo

setTimeout(changeText, 2000)

👉 Result:

  • Pehle: "Chai aur Code"
  • 2 sec baad: "Best JS Series"

🔹 Real Life Example

  • Websites pe popup aata hai after few seconds
  • Discount message show hota hai
  • Auto notification

👉 Ye sab setTimeout se hota hai

🔹 Problem (Important)

❗ Agar timeout lag gaya hai:

  • Usko cancel kaise karein?

👉 Ye production level ka concept hai

🔹 clearTimeout()

📌 Use:

  • setTimeout ko cancel karne ke liye

Step 1: Timeout ko store karo

uska referense store kroge or niche cleartimeout dedena 
apko dhyan rkhna h jha bhi yeh settimeout likhega whi se yeh call stack pe chla jaayega 

const timeoutId = setTimeout(changeText, 2000)

Step 2: clearTimeout use karo

clearTimeout(timeoutId)

👉 Ye tab kaam karega jab:

  • 2 sec complete hone se pehle call ho

🔹 Button se Stop karna

Step 1: Button select karo

document.querySelector("#stop")

Step 2: Event listener lagao

document.querySelector("#stop").addEventListener("click", function(){
    clearTimeout(timeoutId)
    console.log("Stopped")
})

👉 Result:

  • Agar 2 sec se pehle click kiya:
    • Text change nahi hoga

🔹 Summary (Part 1)

  • setTimeout:
    • ek baar run hota hai
  • callback:
    • function jo baad me run hota hai
  • reference pass karo, execute nahi
  • clearTimeout:
    • timeout cancel karta hai
  • Real use:
    • popups, delay actions

👉 Next Part me hum cover karenge:

  • setInterval
  • Continuous execution
  • Stop interval (clearInterval)
  • Project (color changer)

🔹 setInterval kya hota hai?

📌 Definition:

  • setInterval ek function hai jo:
    • same kaam ko bar-bar repeat karta hai
    • ek fixed time interval ke baad

📌 Difference from setTimeout

FeaturesetTimeoutsetInterval
Run count1 baarbar-bar
Use casedelayrepeat task

🔹 Syntax

setInterval(callback, time_in_ms)

🔹 Basic Example

setInterval(function(){
    console.log("Hitesh")
}, 1000)

👉 Result:

  • Har 1 second me "Hitesh" print hota rahega

🔹 Continuous Output Example

setInterval(function(){
    console.log("Hitesh", Date.now())
}, 1000)

👉 Output:

  • Har second ek new timestamp ke saath print hoga

🔹 Important Problem

❗ Ye kabhi rukega nahi
👉 Browser slow bhi ho sakta hai

🔹 clearInterval()

📌 Use:

  • setInterval ko stop karne ke liye

Step 1: Interval store karo

const intervalId = setInterval(function(){
    console.log("Hitesh")
}, 1000)

Step 2: Stop karo ( same clear interrval se stop hojayega ) 

clearInterval(intervalId)

🔹 Parameters bhi pass kar sakte ho

setInterval(function(str){
    console.log(str)
}, 1000, "Hi")

👉 Output:

  • Har second → "Hi"

🔹 Project Setup (Start + Stop Buttons)

HTML:

<button id="start">Start</button>
<button id="stop">Stop</button>

🔹 Goal

  • Start click → interval start
  • Stop click → interval stop

🔹 Step 1: Variable declare karo

let intervalId

🔹 Step 2: Start button logic

document.querySelector("#start").addEventListener("click", function(){
    intervalId = setInterval(function(){
        console.log("Running...")
    }, 1000)
})

🔹 Step 3: Stop button logic

document.querySelector("#stop").addEventListener("click", function(){
    clearInterval(intervalId)
})

👉 Result:

  • Start → console print start
  • Stop → print band

🔥 Mini Project: Background Color Changer

🔹 Goal

  • Start → har second background color change
  • Stop → color change ruk jaye

🔹 Step 1: Random Color Generator

function randomColor(){
    const hex = "0123456789ABCDEF"
    let color = "#"

    for(let i = 0; i < 6; i++){
        color += hex[Math.floor(Math.random() * 16)]
    }

    return color
}

👉 Ye kya karta hai:

  • Hex color generate karta hai
  • Example: #A1B2C3

🔹 Step 2: Background change function

function changeBgColor(){
    document.body.style.backgroundColor = randomColor()
}

🔹 Step 3: Interval logic

let intervalId

Start button:

document.querySelector("#start").addEventListener("click", function(){

    if(!intervalId){
        intervalId = setInterval(changeBgColor, 1000)
    }

})

Stop button:

document.querySelector("#stop").addEventListener("click", function(){

    clearInterval(intervalId)
    intervalId = null

})

🔹 Important Improvement (Very Important 🚀)

❗ Problem:

  • Bar-bar start click karoge → multiple intervals start ho jayenge

✅ Solution:

if(!intervalId){
    intervalId = setInterval(changeBgColor, 1000)
}

👉 Matlab:

  • Agar already chal raha hai → dobara start nahi hoga

🔹 Clean Code Practice

  • Interval stop hone ke baad: 
intervalId = null

👉 Benefit:

  • Memory clean
  • Future bugs avoid

🔹 Final Behavior

  • Start → color change start
  • Stop → freeze color
  • Dubara start → phir se change

🔹 Key Learnings

  • setInterval = repeat task
  • clearInterval = stop repeat
  • Always store interval ID
  • Use safety check (if(!intervalId))
  • Clean code = intervalId = null
